plugin_desc: XKL DXMOS optical transport SNMP child template (enterprise PEN 21150). Beyond standard MIB-II uptime and IF-MIB interface metrics supplied by the shared Transmission SNMP floor, it collects XKL private hardware health tables for sensor temperature, fan controller state and power-supply state. Temperature is reported directly in degrees Celsius. Fan and power-supply status values are normalized so normal(2) maps to 1 healthy and every other state maps to 2 fault. CPU and memory usage are not exposed as stable shared health objects in XKL-MIB, so they are N/A.
name: Transmission

metrics.name: device_temperature_celsius
metrics.description: Highest XKL hardware sensor temperature in degrees Celsius, read directly from xklSensorTemp.

metrics.name: device_fan_state
metrics.description: Worst XKL fan-controller status, normalized from xklFanControllerStatus so normal(2) is healthy and every other state is abnormal.

metrics.name: device_psu_state
metrics.description: Worst XKL power-supply status, normalized from xklPowerStatus so normal(2) is healthy and every other state is abnormal.

metrics.name: interface_ifHCInOctets
metrics.description: This metric indicates the average rate of bytes received over the past 5 minutes, based on the 64-bit ifHCInOctets counter which does not wrap on high-speed interfaces.

metrics.name: interface_ifHCOutOctets
metrics.description: This metric indicates the average rate of bytes sent over the past 5 minutes, based on the 64-bit ifHCOutOctets counter which does not wrap on high-speed interfaces.

metrics.name: snmp_uptime
metrics.description: This metric indicates the uptime of the device since the last restart. By monitoring the uptime, network administrators can understand the stability and reliability of the device and identify potential reboot or failure issues.
